channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Austin’s late-game heroics enable Cal to hold off Belichick’s North Carolina Tar Heels, 21-18
item.description: Austin saved the Bears by punching the ball out of the grasp of a Tar Heels receiver who was about to score a late, go-ahead TD. Austin then recovered the fumble in the end zone.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Faraudo, The Mercury News

item.title: Minnesota beats No. 25 Nebraska 24-6, sacking Dylan Raiola 9 times
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Darius Taylor ran for 148 yards and a touchdown, Drake Lindsey completed 16 of 20 passes for 153 yards and a score and Minnesota sacked quarterback Dylan Raiola a school-record nine times in a 24-6 victory over No. 25 Nebraska on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: PHIL ERVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shohei Ohtani makes MLB history to send Dodgers back to the World Series
item.description: Shohei Ohtani's historic three-homer, 10-strikeout performance sent the Los Angeles Dodgers back to the World Series with a sweep of Milwaukee in the NLCS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Louisville intercepts 4 of Beck's passes, tops No. 2 Miami 24-21 to end Hurricanes' home streak
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Louisville tried a little bit of everything. A fake field goal. Having three different players taking snaps from center. A reverse that wasn’t a reverse. It was enough for the Cardinals to stun No. 2 Miami.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aces celebrate third WNBA championship in 4 years with parade on Las Vegas Boulevard
item.description: Eight years to the day the WNBA and NBA Board of Governors confirmed the relocation of the San Antonio Stars to Las Vegas, the Aces celebrated their third championship in four years with a parade down the famed Strip.
item.dc:creator: W.G. Ramirez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Larkin's overtime goal lifts Red Wings past Lightning, 2-1
item.description: Dylan Larkin scored on a breakaway 3:36 into overtime to give the Detroit Red Wings a 2-1 victory over the Tampa Bay Lightning on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mariners a win from first World Series, beat Blue Jays behind Suárez's grand slam for 3-2 ALCS lead
item.description: Eugenio Suárez hit a go-ahead grand slam after Cal Raleigh’s tying drive in a five-run eighth inning, giving the Seattle Mariners a 6-2 win over the Toronto Blue Jays and a 3-2 AL Championship Series lead.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Destin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Indonesia denies Team Israel travel visas, prompting criticism by International Olympic Committee
item.description: The International Olympic Committee criticized Indonesia after the country refused visas for Israeli gymnasts competing in the World Championships in Jakarta.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill Belichick, Jordon Hudson's hot mic moment leaked amid UNC's drama-filled season
item.description: Leaked footage shows North Carolina coach Bill Belichick and his girlfriend, Jordon Hudson, criticizing a graphics team during the filming of his TV show last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 49ers' Brock Purdy out for third straight game; Mac Jones to start against Falcons
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Quarterback Brock Purdy has been officially ruled out of his third straight game for the San Francisco 49ers with a toe injury and Mac Jones will start against the Atlanta Falcons.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohtani puts on 2-way Sho for the ages with 3 homers and 10 Ks to lead Dodgers back to World Series
item.description: Shohei Ohtani hit three home runs and pitched shutout ball into the seventh inning for the Los Angeles Dodgers, putting on a spectacular two-way show in Game 4 of the National League Championship Series.
item.dc:creator: Greg Beacham, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rashee Rice to play first game for Chiefs in more than a year after suspension
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Rashee Rice knows almost to the day how long it's been since he played in a game for the Kansas City Chiefs. Two more days until he does it again.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turning Point USA calls for NBA employee to be fired for crude remarks about Charlie Kirk's assassination
item.description: Turning Point USA wants the NBA dismiss an employee who had been suspended for posting crude messages on social media regarding Charlie Kirk's assassination.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FIFA announces over 1 million tickets sold for 2026 World Cup in North America
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — More than 1 million tickets have already been sold for next year's World Cup, FIFA said Thursday in its first update on numbers since the official start of sales began earlier this month. The highest demand, as would be expected, were from buyers in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rams rule leading receiver Puka Nacua out with ankle injury for London game against the Jaguars
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Los Angeles Rams star receiver Puka Nacua won't play against the Jacksonville Jaguars in London because of an ankle injury. The Rams ruled out Nacua and right tackle Rob Havenstein on Friday. Havenstein (ankle) will miss his third consecutive games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colts' Zaire Franklin backs suspended Lions safety Brian Branch over JuJu Smith-Schuster fight
item.description: Indianapolis Colts linebacker Zaire Franklin defended Detroit Lions safety Brian Branch for his fight with Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver JuJu Smith-Schuster.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mammoth beat Calgary in home opener, 3-1
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - JJ Peterka and Barrett Hayton scored second-period goals, Karel Vejmelka made 19 saves, and the Utah Mammoth beat the Calgary Flames 3-1 on Wednesday night. Utah became the sixth NHL franchise to win each of its first two home openers.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: Angel Reese becomes first pro athlete to walk in Victoria's Secret Fashion Show
item.description: Angel Reese took her game from the court to the catwalk and made history along the way. The Chicago Sky forward became the first professional athlete to walk in the Victoria’s Secret Fashion Show on Wednesday, joining the brand’s high-profile “Wings Reveal” lineup in New York.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mammoth beat Nashville in overtime, 3-2
item.description: NASHVILLE (ABC4 Sports) - The very first victory for the Utah Mammoth was a dramatic one. Dylan Guenther scored at 2:56 of overtime and the Utah Mammoth rallied for a 3-2 win over the Nashville Predators on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Liberty Flames midfielder's love for soccer stems from Mexican heritage
item.description: LYNCHBURG, Va. (WFXR) -- When it comes to soccer, polls and studies show it's the most popular sport in the Latin American community. For Liberty Flames women's soccer player Gaby Bazan, that love for the sport stems from her Mexican heritage. "Both of my parents grew up with nothing.
item.dc:creator: Jermaine Ferrell, NewsNation

item.title: Monday offers Tyrique Stevenson an opportunity for redemption, one year after the play critics use to define him
item.description: Tyrique Stevenson has used the criticism he received from the "Fail Mary" play to become a better player, and has since earned Pro Football Focus's highest defensive grades among corners in Week 4.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
